column5: CHECK
Is the page fully responsive on all devices, with no design or functionality issues on mobile?
Has the page been tested across different browsers (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Safari) to ensure it displays and functions correctly?
Does the landing page load quickly (ideally under 3 seconds)?
Does the most important information (headline, CTA, hero image) appear above the fold without requiring scrolling?
Does the above-the-fold content communicate what the business does within 5 seconds, ensuring visitors immediately understand the value proposition?
Does the overall page design demonstrate a high level of professionalism and build trust with visitors through thoughtful design, visuals, and messaging?
Is the landing page URL clear, easy to read, and aligned with conventional URL structures to ensure it appears trustworthy?
Is a recognizable favicon present in the browser tab, reinforcing the brand’s professionalism and consistency?
Are notifications or alerts set up for goal completions (e.g., conversions), and is everyone who needs to receive lead information properly included in the notifications?
If present, is the navigation bar simple and have a conventional design (logo top left and CTA top right)? If using links ensure they are section links to different sections of the same page.
Is there a sticky CTA button that follows the user as they scroll?
Is there a trust signal such as a total review count or accreditation logos?
Does the main headline clearly communicate what the page is about and a proposed value proposition?
Is there a supporting subheadline that reinforces the main message and elaborates on the value?
Are there short, direct benefit points or value propositions below the subheadline, clearly communicating the key advantages to the visitor?
Is the hero media visually engaging and aligned with the message? Does it evoke the right emotion, clearly illustrate the product/service, and make it immediately clear what the page is about?
Is the CTA button clearly visible in the hero section (above the fold)? Is it attention-grabbing with clear, actionable language?

Is there a visible trust signal, such as an accreditation (e.g., BBB, TrustPilot) or a total review count with a recognizable review platform logo (e.g., Google Reviews, Yelp) to build credibility right away?
Is there personalised text or a pre-headline that enhances the visitor’s experience by mentioning their location or calling out who they are (e.g., homeowners, business owners)?
Is there a clear, benefit-oriented headline above the form that explains what the user will get by filling out the form (e.g., "Get Your Free Quote Now")?
Does the subheading reinforce the headline by clarifying what will happen next or providing additional context (e.g., "We'll get back to you within 24 hours with a custom estimate")?
Is the form placed in the hero section for easy access? Is it visible above the fold without requiring scrolling?
Is the form positioned in a location where it naturally fits within the flow of the page (e.g., after benefits, near the final pitch)?
Have you kept the number of form fields to a minimum (only asking for essential information)?
If the form requires a lot of information, is it broken into manageable, multi-step stages to prevent overwhelming the user and improve completion rates?
Are questions easy to understand and answer, using dropdowns or checklists wherever possible, instead of open-ended question fields, to minimise friction and decision fatigue?
Are all form fields clearly labelled, with placeholder text or labels that describe the required information?
Are there user-friendly inline error messages to guide visitors if they make a mistake?
Is auto-complete enabled where possible to make the form easier to fill out?
Is the form fully responsive and easy to complete on mobile devices, with appropriately sized fields and buttons?
If the form is multi-step, is there a clear progress indicator to show how many steps are left?
Is the form’s CTA button clear, action-oriented, and visually distinct (e.g., "Get My Free Quote" instead of "Submit")?
Is there any trust signal or social proof (e.g., "Join 10,000+ subscribers," "As seen on...") near the form to build confidence?
Is there a privacy assurance message (e.g., "We won’t spam you" or "Your information is safe with us") near the form to reduce hesitation?
Have you clearly marked optional fields to avoid overwhelming users?
Is there an incentive for form completion (e.g., "Get 10% off" or "Free consultation") to motivate visitors to fill it out?
Is there a confirmation message or redirect to a thank you page that reassures users their form has been submitted successfully, and sets expectations for what happens next?
Is the CTA button text actionable and specific (e.g., "Get Started Now," "Claim Your Free Trial")?
Is the CTA button clearly visible and placed in strategic areas across the page?
Have you placed multiple CTAs throughout the page to capture visitors at different stages of decision-making?
Does the CTA button stand out visually with contrasting colours to make it eye-catching?
Is the copy clear, persuasive, and free of friction (avoid "Submit," "Click Here")?

Does the CTA create a sense of urgency or scarcity where appropriate (e.g., "Limited Time Offer" or "Only 5 Spots Left") to encourage quicker conversions? If not relevant, does the CTA still clearly motivate immediate action?
Is the transformation section placed directly below the hero section to provide an immediate step-by-step visualisation of what happens after the visitor completes the form?
Does the section walk the visitor through a clear, step-by-step process of what happens after purchasing or signing up?
Are clear icons, images, or illustrations used to visually represent each step, making the journey easy to understand at a glance?
Is the transformation section presented in a multi-column layout to make it scannable and visually appealing?
Are the steps clearly labelled (e.g., "Step 1," "Step 2") with concise, actionable explanations of what happens at each stage?
Does the transformation section focus on the positive end result or benefit the visitor will experience after completing the process?
Is the text and imagery emotionally engaging, showing how life or business will improve post-purchase or post-sign-up?
Is there a before-and-after comparison (visual or textual) that illustrates the difference the visitor will experience?
Is the offer immediately clear to the visitor? Can they quickly understand the benefits of taking action?
Does the offer directly address the visitor’s pain points or challenges?
Does the offer emphasise benefits (how it helps) rather than just features (what it is)?
Are there elements of urgency (limited-time offer) or scarcity (limited spots or quantities) to motivate quick action?
Is the value-driven offer presented with clear, supporting visuals that enhance the appeal?

Do testimonials have a design that mimics the original platform they came from (e.g., Google Reviews, Yelp), including star ratings, logos, or reviewer's information, to make them appear more authentic and trustworthy?
Are testimonials used throughout the landing page to support specific content, not just in a dedicated testimonial section, to reinforce key messages and build trust continuously?

Are there high-quality testimonials that highlight key benefits and overcome common objections? Are key quotes or persuasive excerpts from testimonials highlighted (e.g., bolded or used as a headline) to catch attention.
Are there video testimonials to add authenticity?
Have you included detailed case studies that show real-world results?
If applicable, are recognizable client logos displayed to build credibility?
Are metrics like "Over 1,000 satisfied customers" or "Trusted by 500+ businesses" displayed to build trust through social proof?
Are trust badges or symbols (e.g., privacy badges, SSL-secured icons) displayed near the form or CTA to reassure visitors that their personal information is secure?
Are any industry certifications, awards, or accreditations displayed to establish credibility?
Are logos from relevant trade organisations, governing bodies, or professional associations displayed to show industry compliance and professionalism?
Is there a short privacy assurance message near the form (e.g., "We will never share your information")?

Are the benefits presented in a format that’s easy to scan (e.g., icons, imagery, bullet points)? Is a multi-column layout used to organise benefits, making the information easier to digest at a glance?
Does the copy connect with the visitor's emotions by addressing their pain points, desires, and how your product/service will improve their life or solve a problem?
Are the benefits clearly highlighted over the features? Does each feature have a corresponding benefit statement?
Are there visuals, such as diagrams, infographics, or product images, to enhance the explanation of benefits?
Are the product or service features clearly explained and easy to understand?
If applicable, is there a comparative table to show how your offering stands against competitors?
Is there a money-back or satisfaction guarantee near the CTA to reduce risk?
Is a no-commitment offer (e.g., free trial) prominently displayed to reduce risk?
Are there confidence-boosting statements near the CTA (e.g., "No Obligation," "No Credit Card Required")?
Does the FAQ address common concerns, objections, or uncertainties?
Is the FAQ section easy to navigate, with questions grouped logically?
Are the answers concise and directly address the visitor’s concerns?
Does the final pitch section restate the core benefits and reinforce the value proposition?
Is there a final CTA button to encourage conversions at the end of the page?
Is this section visually engaging and consistent with the rest of the page?
Does the final pitch reiterate the core value proposition one last time to reinforce the offer?
Does the footer include clear contact information (phone number, email, etc.)?
Are links to important legal pages, such as the privacy policy, terms and conditions, included?
Is there a secondary, subtle CTA for visitors who scroll to the bottom?
If relevant, are social media links included?
Are trust badges or certifications repeated in the footer for additional reassurance?
Have clear conversion goals been established within your analytics platform or landing page builder (e.g., form submissions, button clicks, phone calls)?
Are events like CTA clicks, form submissions, or video plays being tracked in Google Analytics or other analytics platforms?

column3: Google Ads Tag & Meta Pixel Integrated
column5: Are your Google Ads and Meta Pixel codes properly installed and configured to track conversions from paid campaigns?
If your landing page uses a phone call CTA, is call tracking enabled to measure the source and quality of calls (e.g., WhatConverts, CallRail, Google Ads Call Tracking)?
Is the phone number/area code being used in line with the geographic location or area targeted in the campaign, ensuring it feels familiar and relevant to visitors?
